page: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highcpu-192 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c4n server family. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 384.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, aligning the instance with high-density compute requirements where processing power is prioritized over large memory footprints.

The hardware profile does not include local storage or integrated graphics processing units, meaning all storage requirements must be handled via external network-attached volumes. Additionally, the instance provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This resource allocation makes the instance highly specialized for raw CPU execution, offering high resource density for parallelized workloads while avoiding the added cost of bundled local disks or GPU hardware.

Given these specifications, the c4n-highcpu-192 is suited for compute-intensive workloads such as large-scale parallel processing, high-performance computing simulations, intensive compilation tasks, and batch processing jobs that require massive CPU capacity without demanding high memory-per-core ratios or local storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4n-highcpu-192 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance within the c4n family. It is equipped with 192 dedicated vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture and 384.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance features no local storage and zero GPUs, focusing resources entirely on high-density CPU execution. This hardware profile is designed for workloads that require massive parallel processing power without the overhead of bundled storage or graphics accelerators. It is suited for compute-heavy tasks such as batch processing, large-scale simulations, and compilation workloads.
